project: Lithos MVP2 Part 5 — lithos-enrich Background Worker
description: Implements the lithos-enrich background worker — the asynchronous learning engine that consumes events, performs entity extraction, applies salience decay, consolidates working memory into long-term signals, and runs periodic full sweeps. This is the machinery that makes retrieval improve over time without blocking the request path.

userStories.title: EnrichWorker skeleton — prerequisites and config
userStories.description: As the system, I want the prerequisite changes (FINDING_POSTED payload fix, file-watcher delete fix, enrich_queue attempts column, config additions) so that the EnrichWorker has the infrastructure it needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Update lithos_finding_post handler in server.py to include knowledge_id in the FINDING_POSTED event payload when the parameter is present
Update the file-watcher delete handler in server.py to include the doc id in the NOTE_DELETED event payload before evicting the note from _meta_cache
Add attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 column to enrich_queue schema in StatsStore
Add max_enrich_attempts: int = 3 to LcmaConfig
Update test_enrich_queue_columns to include attempts in expected column set
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker skeleton — event consumer and drain loop
userStories.description: As the system, I want an in-process background worker that subscribes to the event bus and periodically processes enrichment work, so that learning happens asynchronously.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/lithos/lcma/enrich.py with class EnrichWorker
Constructor accepts config: LcmaConfig, event_bus: EventBus, stats_store: StatsStore, edge_store: EdgeStore, knowledge: KnowledgeManager, coordination: CoordinationService

async start(): subscribes to event bus with event_types=[NOTE_CREATED, NOTE_UPDATED, NOTE_DELETED, TASK_COMPLETED, FINDING_POSTED, EDGE_UPSERTED] and maxsize=ENRICH_SUBSCRIBER_QUEUE_SIZE; starts consumer task and drain task via asyncio.create_task
async stop(): cancels tasks, unsubscribes from event bus, graceful shutdown
Helper _resolve_node_id(payload, knowledge, event_type) -> str | None encapsulates resolution and validation logic per the normalization contract
Event consumer handles task.completed events as task-level work (node_id=None, task_id=payload['task_id'])
Event consumer handles edge.upserted events as node-level work for both from_id and to_id, validating each against KnowledgeManager before enqueueing
Event consumer handles note.created/note.updated events resolving doc ID via payload id or path-based resolution via KnowledgeManager.get_id_by_path
Event consumer handles note.deleted events using payload id only, skipping KnowledgeManager existence check
Event consumer handles finding.posted events using payload knowledge_id, validating against KnowledgeManager, skipping when knowledge_id absent
Drain loop runs every config.enrich_drain_interval_minutes; calls drain() which processes pending nodes and tasks from enrich_queue
drain() calls StatsStore.drain_pending_nodes() and StatsStore.drain_pending_tasks() which atomically claim and mark rows as processed at read time
Error handling with requeue: when enrichment fails for a claimed item, worker calls StatsStore.requeue_failed(claimed_ids) resetting processed_at = NULL
Retry cap: drain_pending_nodes()/drain_pending_tasks() only claim rows with attempts < config.max_enrich_attempts; requeue_failed() increments attempts; items exceeding cap logged at WARNING
Worker is started during Lithos server startup when config.lcma.enabled is True and stopped during server shutdown
Unit test: start/stop lifecycle
Unit test: events flow through to enrich_queue
Unit test: edge.upserted event with nonexistent from_id or to_id is dropped and logged at DEBUG
Unit test: finding.posted event with nonexistent knowledge_id is dropped and logged at DEBUG
Unit test: note.deleted event with valid id is enqueued even though node no longer exists in KnowledgeManager
Unit test: drain loop processes and marks items
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker node-level enrichment — salience decay and edge projection
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want to update node salience based on usage patterns and re-sync provenance edges when notes change.
acceptanceCriteria: EnrichWorker._enrich_node(node_id, trigger_types) performs salience decay: reads node_stats, computes days since last_used_at (or last_retrieved_at fallback), applies decay if days > 0
EnrichWorker._enrich_node performs edge projection via new _project_node_provenance(edge_store, knowledge, node_id) helper
reinforce_cited_nodes() in src/lithos/lcma/reinforcement.py (Part 2 US-005) sets last_used_at = now when incrementing cited_count
Add decay_inactive_days: int = 7 to LcmaConfig (minimum days since last use before decay kicks in)
Add sweep_interval_hours: int = 24 to LcmaConfig
Add sweep_startup_delay_minutes: int = 10 to LcmaConfig
Add last_decay_applied_at TIMESTAMP column to node_stats via ALTER TABLE with fallback
After applying decay, set last_decay_applied_at = now

Decay formula: days_since_last_use = (now - last_used_at).days; decay_amount = min(0.1, days_since_last_use * 0.005); salience = clamp(salience - decay_amount, 0.0, 1.0); only applied when days_since_last_use > config.decay_inactive_days
Node enrichment is convergent — running twice in same drain cycle is safe (second run sees up-to-date last_decay_applied_at and skips if already applied today)
Update test_node_stats_columns to include last_decay_applied_at in expected column set
Unit test: verify decay application over time

New helper _project_node_provenance(edge_store, knowledge, node_id) in edges.py — handles node-exists case (upserts matching derived_from edges, removes orphans) and node-absent case (deletes all derived_from edges where from_id == node_id)
Unit test: verify _project_node_provenance for a deleted node removes all derived_from edges with from_id == node_id
Unit test: verify edge projection sync for a single node
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker task-level consolidation — schema and StatsStore methods
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want the consolidation tracking tables and StatsStore methods so that task consolidation can be idempotent across retries.
acceptanceCriteria: Add task_consolidation_log table to stats.db: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_consolidation_log (task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, consolidated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

Add consolidation_edge_ops table to stats.db: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consolidation_edge_ops (task_id TEXT NOT NULL, from_id TEXT NOT NULL, to_id TEXT NOT NULL, applied_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (task_id, from_id, to_id))

Add consolidation_salience_ops table to stats.db: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consolidation_salience_ops (task_id TEXT NOT NULL, node_id TEXT NOT NULL, applied_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (task_id, node_id))
StatsStore.is_task_consolidated(task_id) -> bool returns True if task_id exists in task_consolidation_log
StatsStore.mark_task_consolidated(task_id) -> None inserts into task_consolidation_log with INSERT OR IGNORE
StatsStore.has_consolidation_edge_op(task_id, from_id, to_id) -> bool checks consolidation_edge_ops
StatsStore.record_consolidation_edge_op(task_id, from_id, to_id) -> None inserts with INSERT OR IGNORE
StatsStore.has_consolidation_salience_op(task_id, node_id) -> bool checks consolidation_salience_ops
StatsStore.record_consolidation_salience_op(task_id, node_id) -> None inserts with INSERT OR IGNORE
Update test_schema_has_all_tables and test_recreated_db_has_all_tables to include the three new tables
Unit test: per-target op tables correctly track individual edge and salience operations
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker task-level consolidation — _consolidate_task logic
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want to consolidate working memory into long-term signals when a task completes, reinforcing frequently co-activated nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: _consolidate_task(task_id) performs idempotency check via is_task_consolidated before any work
Reads working memory via StatsStore.get_working_memory(task_id) and identifies frequent nodes with activation_count >= 2

Reinforces edges between frequent node pairs: weight += 0.03 using canonical ordering (from_id <= to_id) and same-namespace-only rule; creates a canonical related_to edge when absent. Per-target idempotency via consolidation_edge_ops table (record-before-write pattern for at-most-once semantics across stats.db and edges.db)

Updates salience for frequent nodes: salience += 0.01 with per-target idempotency via consolidation_salience_ops. The consolidation_salience_ops key is recorded only after the salience write succeeds, in the same stats.db transaction, to prevent retries from double-boosting salience
After all writes succeed, calls mark_task_consolidated(task_id)
Consolidation is idempotent both within a drain cycle and across drain cycles
Unit test: consolidate a task with WM entries, verify edge and salience changes
Unit test: consolidate a task with no WM entries — no-op
Unit test: consolidate an already-consolidated task — verify no additional changes
Unit test: requeue_failed() after partial consolidation — verify replay skips already-applied ops and applies remaining ones
make check passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (consolidation schema and StatsStore methods)

userStories.title: EnrichWorker entity extraction — schema and KnowledgeManager changes
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want entities as a typed field on KnowledgeMetadata and accepted by KnowledgeManager.update(), so that entity extraction can write results via the public API.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'entities' to _KNOWN_METADATA_KEYS in knowledge.py so it is not captured in extra
Add entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) to KnowledgeMetadata
Add entities to KnowledgeMetadata.to_dict(): include in output when non-empty
Add entities parsing to KnowledgeMetadata.from_dict(): entities=data.get('entities', [])

KnowledgeManager.update() accepts entities: list[str] | None | _UnsetType = _UNSET parameter; when not _UNSET, replaces metadata.entities. Thread through existing frontmatter write path. Do not add a new KnowledgeManager.write() method
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker entity extraction — _extract_entities logic
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want to auto-extract entity names from notes using rule-based heuristics and populate the entities frontmatter field.
acceptanceCriteria: _extract_entities(node_id) reads note content and extracts entity names using a simple rule-based heuristic approach (e.g., capitalized noun phrases, [[wiki-links]], backtick-enclosed terms)
No LLM dependency — extraction is purely rule-based in MVP2
Extracted entities written to note's entities frontmatter via KnowledgeManager.update(..., entities=extracted) only if entities field is currently absent or empty
Agent-written entities values are never overwritten by the enrichment worker (reads current value first, skips if non-empty list)
Entity extraction called during _enrich_node() only when trigger_types contains note.created or note.updated
Unit test: extract entities from a note with known entities, verify frontmatter update
Unit test: verify agent-written entities are not overwritten
make check passes

userStories.title: EnrichWorker full sweep — decay, WM eviction, provenance reconciliation
userStories.description: As the enrichment worker, I want a periodic full sweep that recomputes decay across all nodes, evicts stale working memory, and reconciles provenance edges.
acceptanceCriteria: full_sweep() loads all node_id values from node_stats via StatsStore.list_all_node_ids()
Applies salience decay to each node using same formula as _enrich_node
Evicts working memory: calls StatsStore.evict_working_memory() with completed/cancelled task IDs and ttl_days=config.wm_eviction_days
Reconciles provenance edges: calls existing whole-store _project_provenance_to_edges(edge_store, knowledge) from edges.py
Sweep loop runs every config.sweep_interval_hours hours (default 24); first run scheduled config.sweep_startup_delay_minutes after startup (default 10)
Full sweep logs summary: 'Full sweep: decayed N nodes, evicted M WM entries, provenance edges created=C removed=R'
StatsStore.list_all_node_ids() -> list[str] added (simple SELECT node_id FROM node_stats)
Unit test: full sweep decays nodes and evicts WM entries
Unit test: full sweep repairs a stale derived_from edge
make check passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (decay logic), US-005 (consolidation), US-007 (entity extraction)
